titlestmt.titleproper: David Alan Richards Collection of Rudyard Kipling
Richards (David Alan) Collection of Rudyard Kipling
titlestmt.author: Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 123703090.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday April 3, 2025 at 23:00
Updated by Tina Evans and Elise Riley to describe February 2024 accession.
Updated by Tina Evans, Elise Riley, and Rosemary K. J. Davis to add recent acquisitions.

did.unittitle: David Alan Richards Collection of Rudyard Kipling

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eighteen groupings: May 2004 Acquisitions, February 2005 Acquisition, October 2008 Acquisition, November 2008 Acquisition, October 2009 Acquisition, November 2010 Acquisition, September 2011 Acquisition, November 2013 Acquisition, August 2014 Acquisition, November 2014 Acquisition, October 2015 Acquisition, December 2016 Acquisition, July 2017 Acquisition, September 2019 Acquisition, September 2021 Acquisition, March 2022 Acquisition, November 2022 Acquisition, November 2022 Acquisition, and February 2024 Acquisition.

bioghist.head: Rudyard Kipling (1865-1936)
bioghist.p: Rudyard Kipling (1865-1936), the English author, was born in Bombay, India, on 30 December 1865, to John Lockwood Kipling, professor in the Sir Jamsetjee Jeejeebhoy School of Art in Bombay, India, and his wife, Alice (Macdonald) Kipling. Kipling was sent to England at the age of five, where he boarded and schooled until he took a job at a newspaper, the Civil and Military Gazette , in Lahore, Pakistan, in 1882. He started publishing stories in the 1880s while working at the Gazette and later at the Pioneer . He returned to England briefly in 1889 and then travelled and settled in the United States for several years. He continued to write prolifically during the 1890s, and by the time he returned to England in late 1896, he was already very well-known. In 1902 Kipling settled at "Bateman's" in Burwash, East Sussex, where he resided until his death on 18 January 1936.
Additional biographical information can be found in standard print and online sources.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection received a basic level of processing in 2015, including rehousing and in some instances minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, identified by month and year of acquisition.

Descriptive information found in the Collection Contents section is drawn in large part from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during processing.
Former call numbers: Uncat Ms Vault File, Uncat MSS 743, Uncat MSS 1047, Uncat MSS 1236, Uncat MSS 1255, and Uncat MSS 1292.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The David Alan Richards Collection of Rudyard Kipling consists of material by and relating to English author Rudyard Kipling. There are autograph and typescript letters, signed, from Kipling to others as well as some third-party letters relating to Kipling or the collection. Correspondents include Edward Bok, Ladies Home Journal , Sir Philip Burne-Jones, Moncure D. Conway, C. S. Jarvis, John Lockwood Kipling, A. E. W. Mason, H. Cotton Minchin, S. S. McClure Company, E. W. Stoddard, and others. Writings include a corrected typescript draft of Kipling's first novel, The Light that Failed (1890), an autograph manuscript draft and illustrations for The Princess in the Pickle-bottle , and autograph manuscript and typescript drafts of a number of poems. In addition, there are illustrated works by Kipling's father, John Lockwood Kipling, and a bibliography of Kipling's work. Other papers include scrapbooks, consisting chiefly of clippings, photographs, postcards and other printed ephemera, artwork, and memorabilia relating to Kipling.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The David Alan Richards Collection of Rudyard Kipling is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

persname.part: Mason, A. E. W. (Alfred Edward Woodley), 1865-1948

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The 2004 Acquisitions consists of letters from Rudyard Kipling to others.
Former call number: Uncat Ms Vault Kipling.

did.unittitle: Letters to the Editor of "The Merry Thought" and a clipping

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The 2005 Acquisition consists of a corrected draft of Kipling's first novel, The Light that Failed , bound together with an autograph letter, signed, from Kipling to an unidentified editor at Lippincott's Magazine .
Former call number: Uncat MSS 743.

did.unittitle: The Light that Failed , draft, typescript and typescript carbon, corrected, with proofreaders' marks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Former call number: Uncat MSS 1047.

did.unittitle: Autograph letter, signed, from Kipling to [Anthony Hope Hawkins]
"Bibliography of Works by Rudyard Kipling" by Lloyd Chandler, autograph manuscript and typescript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Former call number: Uncat Ms Vault File.

did.unittitle: Autograph letter, signed, from Kipling to "Lady Sackville"
Typed letter, signed, from E. M. Blaikie to H. F. H. Caldwell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Former call number: Uncat MSS 1236.

did.unittitle: The Princess in the Pickle-bottle , illustrations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Extracted from: The World. New York : Sunday, October 1891.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The November 2010 Acquisition features Mary C. Ensor's papers relating the Kipling family. Mary C. Ensor's father was the Kipling family doctor and Mary received drawing lessons from John Lockwood Kipling. The Ensor papers include correspondence, an autograph book, and numerous original drawings by John Lockwood Kipling. There are approximately eighteen autograph letters, signed, from different members of the Kipling family to Mary and Dr. Ensor.
Former call number: Uncat MSS 1255.

did.unittitle: Autograph letters, signed, from Kipling to others
Mary C. Ensor Collection of Kipling

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The 2011 Acquisition includes correspondence, drafts of writings, and photographs. There are autograph and typescript letters, signed, from Kipling to Edward Bok, editor of the Ladies Home Journal , Shane Chichester, and others, as well as a third-party letter from A. E. W. Mason to Doubleday and Company. Writings include a corrected autograph manuscript draft by Kipling of "The Last Rhyme of True Thomas," circa 1894, and "The Rhyme of Johnny Grundy and His Grey Mare" by John Lockwood Kipling. The latter work, dated "Bombay, January 1869," is illustrated.
There are photographs of Kipling, Kipling's wife Caroline, his parents, and others.
Former call number: Uncat MSS 1292.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 7 leaves from: The Sun. New York: 1897: Jan 24, Jan 31, Feb 7, Feb 14, Feb 21, Feb 28, Mar 7.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sixteen pages from the Montreal daily star, v. 48: 123 (1916 May 24). Label: Library of Congress. Accompanied by: certificate of authentication, 1 L.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One page from the San Francisco Sunday Chronicle.

did.unittitle: Jutland battle told by Kipling in verse and prose: clipping

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Eighteen pages from the New York Times, v. 66: 21,453 (1916 Oct 19). Accompanied by: Historic Newspapers invoice, 1 L.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One sheet from the Pittsburgh Press (1892 May 8). Accompanied by: certificate of authentication, invoice from Historic Newspapers, business card, 3 L.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One sheet from the New York Sunday news. 1892 Apr 24.

did.unittitle: Mr. R. Kipling speaks up: clipping

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One sheet from The World, v. 32: 11,219 (1892 May 8). Accompanied by: certificate of authentication, 1 L.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One sheet from the Chicago Sunday Times (1894 Dec 2). Laid on rice paper.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subdivisions for Writings and Other Material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The November 2013 Acquisition contains drafts and other material relating to writings, a notebook, photographs, memorabilia, and commemorative material relating to Rudyard Kipling.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Include's "The Mother of the Man" by Hall Caine, typescript

did.unittitle: The Light that Failed , "Censorship Dialogue Script" for film adaptation, typescript mimeograph
"The Literary Beggar" by Sparham Sheldrake, poem, autograph manuscript, signed
The Princess in the Pickle-bottle , illustrations
Pen and ink portrait of Kipling by Stanley D. Lovegrove, signed and framed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 49 x 31 cm, matted to 69 x 50, covered in plastic protector, color photogcopy of another similar broadside taped to verso of mat. Smaller illustrations of artist, author and soldier aiming his rifle undnerneath the Gentleman in Kharki. Autographs: R. Caton Woodville; Rudyard Kipling.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The August 2014 Acquisition contains writings, correspondence, artwork, clippings and printed ephemera, photographs, scrapbooks, and other papers by or relating to Rudyard Kipling. Correspondence includes letters from Kipling to E. W. Stoddard, the publishers S. S. McClure Company, Charles Oman, and others, as well as third-party letters from A. E. W. Mason to Doubleday and Company, from his father John Lockwood Kipling to Monsure D. Conway, and from Sir Philip Burne Jones to Lucy Clifford.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 3 studies bu F. H. Townsend for "The Brushwood Boy", 1907, with autograph note, signed, to Townsend from Kipling; and 2 drawings for "All the Mowgli Stories" by Stuart Tresilion.

did.unittitle: "Supplication of the Black Aberdeen," poem, typescript carbon, corrected
"To Oblige a Young American Lady," autograph manuscript note, signed
"Bobs," poem, autograph manuscript and printed version
[5 War Poems], drafts, typescript, corrected, and page proofs
Kipling copyright case archive of Augustus Gurlitz
"The Princess in the Pickle-bottle," autograph manuscript, signed and framed
"The Recruit's Progress," draft, autograph manuscript, corrected
Autograph letter, signed from Kipling to C. Gallop, with portrait engraving of Kipling, framed
Colored print portrait of Kipling by David Levine, framed
Scrapbook on Kipling assembled by E. T. D. Francis

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by material type and title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The 2014 Addition contains the following material: two autograph letters, signed, from Kipling to others; two autograph manuscript drafts; a printing plate for "The King's Ankus," several photographs of Kipling and family, including his parents and daughters, Josephine and Elsie; and his sister's, Alice (Trix), fan, signed by numerous people during the early 1890s.

did.unittitle: Autograph letter, signed, from Kipling to Fred M. Hopkins
Autograph letter, signed, from Kipling to "Lizzie"
"Puck [of?] Pook's Hill," poem, autograph manuscript, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Single leaf [removed from September? 1918? issue] laminated, 1 L. In ms: 1918 "German Kultur - What it means - Help destroy it. By Rudyard Kipling"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Single laminated leaf removed from Sunday, December 9, 1900 issue, 1 L. "Kipling's American home for sale. His last tie with this country to be severed"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, dated April 11, 1895, from Rudyard Kipling to "Dear Clarke," and one autograph letter, signed, from John Lockwood Kipling to "Dear Margot."

did.unittitle: Autograph letter, signed, from Rudyard Kipling to "Dear Clarke"
Autograph letter, signed, from J. L. Kipling to "Dear Margot"
"The legs of Sister Ursula," draft, autograph manuscript, corrected and signed
Facsimile of autograph letter, signed, dated March 7 1896, from Kipling to Fred M. Hopkins
Photostat of autograph letter, signed, dated October 26 1887, from Kipling to an unidentified recipient
Typescript letter, signed, from Chandler to "Dear Maitland"
"Talk on the Kipling Society in the United States... by L.H. Chandler"
"Kipling versus Fenno: A complication of extracts from the legal papers covering this suit"
Copies of Circuit Court documents relating to "Kipling v. Fenno"
Autograph manuscript letter, initialed, to "My dear McClure"
Autograph note, signed, from Julian Ralph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accession consists of seven volumes gathering photocopies of printed versions and drafts of Kipling poetry assembled by Thomas Pinney during production of The Cambridge edition of the poems of Rudyard Kipling and five black-and-white photographic prints from the 1937 Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer production of the film Captains Courageous , including Lionel Barrymore and Spencer Tracy.

did.unittitle: "First printings and original manuscript versions of Rudyard Kipling's poetry," volumes 1-4, printed versions and drafts (photocopies), annotated
"First printings and original manuscript versions of Rudyard Kipling's poetry," volumes 5-7, printed versions and drafts (photocopies), annotated

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This acquisition consists of correspondence, related papers, and a photograph of Kipling.

did.unittitle: Martindell letter to A.J. Hoppe with proofs and illustrations for A Bibliography of the Works of Rudyard Kipling
Emile Otto Hoppé photograph of Kipling
Kipling letter to Sibyl Healey about illness, pets, and selling his manuscripts
Kipling letter to Alfred Milner, matted with a print of a sketch of Kipling

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This acquisition contains of three pen-and-ink drawings by Stuart Tresilian for All the Mowgli stories (1933) and Animal stories (1932).

did.unittitle: "Poem: Law of the jungle, top of page 127," pen-and-ink drawing

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials largely maintain order found at time of acquisition.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 33 (microfilm):Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 34 (electronic files): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession contains galleys and proofs, photographs, microfilm, and printed material including clippings, menus, trade cards, and stamps relating to Rudyard Kipling.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files contain correspondence removed from electronic media folder and article by David Alan Richards.
